sources.title: 1979 - Delirium - The Monster is Man, when justice goes rogue - grindhouse psycho thriller
sources.description:
A troubled Vietnam vet navigates the concrete jungle of post-war America, where haunting memories blur with present-day struggles. Watch as city streets transform into psychological landscapes in this tense character study that doubles as a snapshot of 70s indie filmmaking!

Directed by: Peter Maris  
Screenplay by: Richard Yalem & Peter Maris  
Story by: Eddie Krell & Jim Loew  
Cinematography: John Arthur Morrill  

⭐️STARRING
Turk Cekovsky - Paul Dollinger  
Debi Chaney - Susan Norcross  
Terry TenBroek - Larry Mead  
Barron Winchester - Eric Stem

Original release: July 19, 1979 
Filmed in: Clayton, Missouri
Genres: Action, Horror, Thriller


https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL2gsu7VGzgdYTMq_LE7cyJT3fio8pHOP8

@CultCinemaClassics

metadata.Plot: Charlie is a psycho; his twisted mind drives him to butcher beautiful young women. Tormented by his past, he brutally murders again and again. He mush be stopped. But how? Hidden beneath the streets of the city, a secret committee of leading citizens has formed a vigilante council. Taking the law into their own hands, they hunt down people they feel have escaped justice. This kangaroo court, led by a professional killer, hides beneath the cloak of respectability, protected by its influential members. Many, perhaps innocent, people have been executed by this invisible and misdirected group, but they made a fatal error. They hired Charlie.
metadata.Writer: Eddie Krell, Jim Loew, Peter Maris
metadata.Actors: Turk Cekovsky, Debi Chaney, Terry TenBroek
